title: Voting rights and models of voter behavior
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on voting rights and models of voter behavior
topic_path: Voting rights and models of voter behavior

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which constitutional change in the early twentieth century led to an increase in democratic participation in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: States no longer could segregate public schools under the principle of “separate but equal”
States’ use of poll taxes to keep African Americans from voting was disallowed
The minimum legal voting age was reduced from 21 to 18
The method by which US senators are elected was changed

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these statements best explains why the Voting Rights Act of 1965 has been important to expanding political participation in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It enforced the Fifteenth Amendment by preventing states from using legal barriers to prevent African Americans from voting
It ended segregation in public places and banned employment discrimination based on race, color, religion, gender, or national origin
It introduced the requirement of showing an official form of identification to register for or vote in an election to deter voter fraud
It reduced the minimum legal age to vote from 21 to 18

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/33223ea94397716a18505cc53efcf7fe.png)

"US Popular Vote for President as Percentage of Total Population." Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=53252605) 

**Referring to the years 1920 to 1940 on the graph, which of the following was a catalyst for the upward trend in the presidential popular vote as a percent of the population in that period?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Giving women the right to vote
Banning poll taxes used to prevent African Americans from voting
Reducing the voting age for elections
Changing the method by which US senators were elected

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/33223ea94397716a18505cc53efcf7fe.png)

"US Popular Vote for President as Percentage of Total Population." Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=53252605) 

Even with the Civil Rights Movement and legislation supporting the Fifteenth Amendment, it was widely reported that, throughout the 1950s, 1960s, and early 1970s, many southern states continued practices that prevented African Americans from voting. 

**Which of the following trends in the graph supports this claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The percentage of voters in presidential elections during these years dropped by more than ten percent.
The percentage of voters in presidential elections during these years increased by almost ten percent
The percentage of voters in presidential elections initially increased and then dropped sharply at the end of the 1960s
The percentage of voters in presidential elections during these years initially declined and then increased slightly

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best explains how the 17th Amendment increased democratic participation in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It changed the election process for US senators
It changed the minimum voting age from 21 to 18
It extended the right to vote to women
It extended the right to vote to black men

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios would most likely be considered a violation of the Twenty-fourth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A state institutes a fee to vote in a presidential election
A state institutes a law requiring state-issued photo identification to vote
A court refuses to provide a lawyer for a defendant who cannot afford one
A court sentences a defendant to life in prison for stealing a loaf of bread

assessment_items.question_content:
Gabriel Sarwan only votes for political candidates whose platforms directly benefit the profitability of his small business. 

**This voting behavior can best be characterized by which of the following models?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Party-line voting
Prospective voting
Retrospective voting
Rational choice voting

assessment_items.question_content:
Priyanka Kumble votes for political candidates based solely on their platforms and the policies they and their party plan to implement once elected. 

**Which model of voting behavior does this best represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Party-line voting
Prospective voting
Retrospective voting
Rational choice voting

assessment_items.question_content:
Rumana Modi votes for political candidates whose rhetoric and policy initiatives while serving in office align with what she wants to be done in the future.

**Which model of voting behavior does this best represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Party-line voting
Prospective voting
Retrospective voting
Rational choice voting

assessment_items.question_content:
Selina voted for Senator Bruce Wayne (D-NY) in 2008 and was unhappy with his performance in office. So, in the 2014 midterm elections, Selina decided to vote for Barbara Kean, a Republican candidate. 

**Which model of voting behavior does this best represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Retrospective voting
Prospective voting
Rational choice voting
Party-line voting

assessment_items.question_content:
Tom Haverford has never voted in an election before, but is excited about Leslie Knope’s campaign promises and decides to support her in the 2020 presidential election in hopes that she will be able to achieve those policy goals.

**Which model of voting behavior does this best represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Prospective voting
Rational choice voting
Party-line voting
Retrospective voting

assessment_items.question_content:
Teddy Flood lives in Texas and is voting in the 2018 election. When he gets to the ballot box, he decides to do straight-ticket voting, in which he checks one box to cast a ballot for every candidate from the Democratic party. 

**Which model of voting behavior does this best represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Party-line voting
Rational choice voting
Prospective voting
Retrospective voting
